title: Long Tasks, Compaction, Steering, and Handoffs · Prompt Lens: Compaction Ready
summary: Keep a multi-hour repository task coherent across context changes and user steering. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt compaction ready increment in a durable task state and handoff record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store durable task details in files or structured summaries before context compaction.
Maintain objective, decisions, evidence, plan status, changed files, tests, and blockers in a compact state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Compaction Ready
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt long context. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt long context. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Compaction Ready contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for store durable task details in files or structured summaries before context compaction.

content.text: Store durable task details in files or structured summaries before context compaction. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt long context. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Compaction Ready, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a durable task state and handoff record.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Compaction always preserves minor but critical constraints. This fails the Compaction Ready contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Compaction Ready evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-compaction-ready-lqpd06" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Compaction Ready into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-compaction-ready-lqpd06
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Compaction Ready model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Compaction Ready applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Store durable task details in files or structured summaries before context compaction.
